Generated Test Suite — extraction + chunked LLM generation only.
This builds steps 1-2 of the feature: pull real, already-analyzed evidence
for a repo (routes, UI elements, UI->backend flows, missing-error-handling
signals) and turn it into a downloadable pytest+Playwright test file via
OpenAI. It deliberately does NOT execute the generated file — running
LLM-generated code automatically is a separate, sandboxed step to be added
later once generation itself is reviewed and stable.
Flask's most common pattern: @app.route("/path", methods=["POST"]) — defaults to
GET when `methods=` is absent, and can declare several methods on one decorator,
which is why this is handled separately from the single-method patterns above.
Deliberately does not try to regex-capture the full `(...)` call up to its closing
paren: methods=("GET", "POST") has its own closing paren before the call's, so a
naive [^)]* group truncates there. Instead this only anchors on the path string;
the methods=(...) lookup runs separately against a plain text window after the match.

Mixpanel activity integration (parallel workflow to Google Analytics).
GA auth is per-browser-session OAuth with no natural persistence, so GA
connection state lives in memory (GOOGLE_ANALYTICS_SESSIONS). Mixpanel uses
static project/service-account credentials instead, which do need to
persist across page loads, so those are stored in Supabase, encrypted.
Loaded events are NOT stored in Supabase (GA doesn't do that either) -
they're written as a generated markdown doc into src/codeval_generated/,
the same mechanism GA uses so events become searchable/answerable
alongside code through the existing /search pipeline.
Unlike GA/Mixpanel custom event properties, Sentry stack frames are exact
(filename, line, function), so errors are stored as real rows in
codeval_analytics_events and matched deterministically against the SCIM
function index + callgraph, instead of going through semantic search.
Email delivery uses Mailjet's HTTPS Send API, which works on Render free services.
Current-file scope means the selected file is the anchor. Keep direct
cross-file calls so a file with no same-file callees does not collapse
to a single node, but only expand traversal through selected-file nodes.
Generic words the callers/callees regex can otherwise swallow as a fake symbol name
(e.g. "find all callers of this function" with no real target captures "function").
Vendor-specific credential shapes that are unambiguous enough to flag at high confidence
from raw text alone, regardless of source language.
A credential-sounding variable assigned a literal string instead of read from the environment.
Requires a quote immediately after `=`/`:`, which already excludes the common safe pattern
of reading from os.environ / process.env (no literal quote follows the operator there).
A DB connection string with embedded credentials, found in a file type that ships to the browser.
CORS configured to allow any origin: the word origin/allow_origins/Access-Control-Allow-Origin
followed within a short window by a quoted "*", which covers FastAPI/Starlette, Flask-CORS,
Express `cors`, and manually-set headers without needing a per-framework parser.
` connection string with embedded credentials appears in a file that ships to the browser.",
match always ends with the literal `{` that opens the body; using
the first `{` in the match would instead catch a destructured
param's brace (e.g. `({ session }) => {`), collapsing the scope.
Safe dynamic SQL shape: only the number of parameter placeholders is interpolated.
Safe dynamic SQL shape: a list of constant parameterized clauses is joined, while values remain bound separately.

Windows MAX_PATH is 260 chars; deeply nested archives (e.g. .gradle
caches) routinely exceed this even with sanitized components. The
\\?\ prefix opts into the extended-length path API so makedirs/open
don't fail with a misleading FileNotFoundError.
